full_text:
Reporting of tCycIF Image processing

Materials and experiment to Report

Samples; number of slides and number of samples, section thickness

Panel&experiment; number of cycles, number of succeed and failed (a list) markers (Check experimental reporting table)

Metadata; pixel scale, binning, scanning magnification

Image processing

Perform illumination correction (default) (optional: you can check the results with Avivator)

Stitching and registration with Ashlar

Go through each sample one by one in Napari and visually check and Report

Stitching verification: That all tiles are registered in all cycles

Marker order verification: The cycles are registered in the right order - correct looking marker expression in each cycle

Registration verification: tiles are aligned correctly: check 1st and last cycle

In practice - can be done in your laptop using Napari

open one sample, check the channels one by one and check that i) signal matches the marker ii) no tiles are missing in any marker

Overlay DAPI signal from 1st to last cycle - 1st cycle in red, last in green and confirm that you have only yellow nuclei

-> repeat steps 1 and 2 for all sample one by one

Segmentation

Run nuclear segmentation with Stardis/Mesmer

Report; which cycle you used for nuclear segmentation (we usually use 1st cycle DAPI)

Open every image in Napari one by one

Overlay masks to the image and compare DAPI signal with masks for potential over or undersegmentation

Check especially dense areas such as TLSs if they are segmented

in a “good-enough” way - it will never be perfect

-> If you encounter significant (>10%) number of cells missegmented you should start to troubleshoot

Potential sources for over or undersegmentation

Tissue detachment

Weak DAPI signal

Dense areas of cells or tissue folds

Quantification (separate script in the pipeline)

Report; Total numbers of cells in dataset and per sample;

Quality Control with Cylinter

Report; which modules you ran

Default; AggregateData, selectROIs, intensityFilter, areaFilter (everything before logTransform if planning to have your own data transformation or normalization) -> save raw data

Continue pipeline if you are happy with logTransfrom

For selecROIs ->Instructions from Saun

Report; Number and % of cells lost after Quantification in dataset and per sample

Check visually which cells were removed, especially in samples with highest cell losses: in Napari randomly visualize 10% of samples by overlaying segmentation masks with the post QC cells from csvs (For future: we need a script to name all cells in the post-QC csv as “kept”, then overlap on masks in Napari, so you can see if cells are removed or not)
